title: Debt Danger Quiz - How Much Risk Is Your Debt Creating? | CrunchMilk
metaDescription: Find out how dangerous your debt situation is with this free 8-question quiz. Assess your debt types, payment habits, and risk level for financial trouble.
tagline: Is your debt under control, or is it a ticking time bomb?

questions.question: How many different debts do you currently have (credit cards, loans, etc.)?

options.text: 5 or more separate debts
3-4 debts
1-2 debts
No debt or only a mortgage

questions.question: What's the highest interest rate on any of your debts?

options.text: Over 24% (store cards, payday loans)
18-24% (typical credit cards)
8-18% (personal loans, older credit cards)
Under 8% or no high-interest debt

questions.question: How do you typically handle your credit card balances?

options.text: I only make minimum payments and balances keep growing
I pay minimums plus a little extra sometimes
I pay significantly more than minimums each month
I pay the full balance every month

questions.question: What percentage of your take-home pay goes to debt payments each month?

options.text: Over 40% - it's suffocating
25-40% - it's a heavy burden
10-25% - manageable but noticeable
Under 10% or debt-free

questions.question: Have you ever missed a payment or paid late in the last 12 months?

options.text: Yes, multiple times - I'm behind on payments
Yes, once or twice
No, but it's been close
Never - all payments are automated and on time

questions.question: Are you taking on new debt while trying to pay off existing debt?

options.text: Yes, my total debt is growing each month
I occasionally add new charges but mostly pay down
I've stopped adding new debt completely
I only use credit for planned purchases I pay off immediately

questions.question: How is your debt affecting your daily stress and decisions?

options.text: I lose sleep over it and avoid looking at my accounts
It causes frequent stress and limits my choices
I'm aware of it but it doesn't dominate my thoughts
Debt is minimal and doesn't affect my quality of life

questions.question: Do you have a specific plan for becoming debt-free?

options.text: No plan - I just try to keep up with payments
I have a vague goal but no specific strategy
I'm following a payoff method (snowball or avalanche)
I have a detailed plan with target payoff dates for each debt

scoreRanges.label: Danger Zone
scoreRanges.analysis: Your debt situation is critical and requires immediate action. Consider reaching out to a nonprofit credit counselor, and focus on stopping new debt accumulation first. The snowball method can help you build momentum with quick wins.

scoreRanges.label: Warning Signs
scoreRanges.analysis: Your debt is manageable but trending in a risky direction. Create a specific payoff plan targeting your highest-interest debt first, and build a small emergency fund to avoid adding more debt when surprises hit.

scoreRanges.label: Under Control
scoreRanges.analysis: You're handling your debt responsibly. Keep your payoff plan on track and consider accelerating payments with any extra income. You're in a position to become debt-free faster than you might think.

scoreRanges.label: Debt-Free Lifestyle
scoreRanges.analysis: Excellent debt management. You either have no debt or manage it strategically with low interest rates and full control. Keep using credit wisely and redirect money that might go to interest toward building wealth instead.
